examples.title: Basic source diff viewer
examples.description: Unified patch output with file headers, hunk headers, and source line states.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { SourceDiffViewer } from '@lostgradient/cinder/source-diff-viewer';

  const patch = `diff --git a/src/example.ts b/src/example.ts
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/src/example.ts
+++ b/src/example.ts
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 export function greet(name: string) {
-  return \`Hello, \${name}.\`;
+  const normalizedName = name.trim();
+  return \`Hello, \${normalizedName}.\`;
 }
`;
</script>

<SourceDiffViewer {patch} />

examples.title: Bounded source diff viewer
examples.description: Large patches can cap rendered diff rows while preserving the total count.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { SourceDiffViewer } from '@lostgradient/cinder/source-diff-viewer';

  const patch = `diff --git a/src/generated.txt b/src/generated.txt
--- a/src/generated.txt
+++ b/src/generated.txt
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 unchanged
-old one
+new one
-old two
+new two
 unchanged
`;
</script>

<SourceDiffViewer {patch} maxLines={4} />
